What's up? Okay, this is gonna be a nugget filled video. So here's the deal. I'm making this video because I want you to be able to self-diagnose because most of the time when you tell me that there's an issue saying like I don't have the numbers that I want, it's one of these four problems and then within one of these four, sorry, it's one of these four issues and then within each of those issues there's three things that could make that a problem. It's less. I try and bump it up. All right, but 30 leads a day is what I'm shooting for. Okay, so 30 days is what I want. So the first thing you do is you can increase your spend. Okay, which is usually what the solution is. It's like increase your spend to get the assets that are doing well. Because when you do that, you're just going to get more leads, right? And since the ROI on the funnel is so good on the front end and from a cash flow standpoint, it kind of just becomes irrelevant. So you just spend as much as you need to get that many leads a day. And the only reason I do that is that's usually the round the point where people stop being able to effectively manage leads. They start wasting weight. So the conversion percentage goes down, which then means you're evening out. So you want to basically get that sweet spot where you're doing as much work as you can without overworking or like having too many leads and then the quality goes down because then you're still paying more per acquisition, which is the important point. So it's really fast acquisition that we're looking at, but 30 days tends to be the sweet spot. So first thing you do is increase your spend. Now, if you're like, my lead cost is outrageous, it's, first of all, your conversion percentage will make any lead cost not outrageous. But if you're like, my lead cost is outrageous, it's $25 a lead or something, which would still work. But we'll just continue. So your lead cost is crazy. Then you just need to refresh the creative or you need to alternate your offers. Okay. Have a different offer for four weeks and then come back. All right. So those are the three things you do to fix lead flow. Usually it's number one, just spend one. All right. Second one. So lead flow is good. But speed to contact is like, you want to contact every single lead within 10 minutes. Like if you're not doing that, then that's probably one of your problems. And then the second problem is that you're not really reaching out enough. You feel like you're reaching out a lot. You spend an hour a day calling, but that's nothing. Like she said four hours a day calling and I guarantee you'll three extra, your sales guaranteed, guaranteed. All right. Some of you have heard my 200 call challenge, which really should be a challenge because it's like, that's what I tell my guys to do every day. But like, if you're not doing it, like make sure, like try doing 200 calls per day and see how many sales you make the next day. And then all of a sudden like realize like the amount of calls I make will make me an extra $3,000 tomorrow. It's worth me making three hours to make an extra three grand. Right. Okay. Right. So if you want to put the scarcity on that, listen, we got too many people were trying to sign up with us. All right. I don't even know if I can get you a spot. That means I have an abundance and they have a scarcity. You don't want it to be the other way around where they're like, listen, there's a million gems I can go to. Why should I go with yours? Right. You see the flip? That's what you want to make sure that you're putting that into them right as soon as they walk in the door. So that you're framing them, you're positioning the conversation in such a way that you're an advantage. Okay. So sketchy, urgency, pre-framing. And like, I'm going to give you the things that like I'm actually thinking about. Like usually the actual pitch, like if you're following the structure of the framework that we have for the actual like sales conversation, you should be fine. But it's, it's the things that are unspoken that are the ones that make the biggest difference. And your conviction as to whether or not you're helping people, whether or not, like, basically how successful do you think people are going to be with this program? If you're doing it to make money, you won't make sales. I promise you. All right. Now, obviously you will make money for doing it, but that can't be your motivation. I'm just, I'm just saying after having done this a long time, like if you can, if you keep their needs first, you will sell way more because they can feel that you're looking out for them and not for your pocketbook or, you know, your wallet, whatever. So make sure that your belief that you're helping them is, is right. And a lot of times people, you know, like if you're doing a promotion of mine or whatever, you just don't know if it's going to help people. If you need that conviction, let me know. I have 2,000 before and after pictures that I stopped and I stopped taking them that like you're one and a half because I just didn't need any more transformations. I had so many it was irrelevant. I just let people take their own pictures. But once you have that conviction, once you've seen it work time and time and time and time again, you start to speak with more conviction because you genuinely believe it. Now, I know it's hard to like have conviction without seeing the result. I'm just telling you the more of this you have, the more you'll sell. Okay. So conviction, rapport. Now, each of these things, like if you have enough with any one of these things, it's, it can overcome crappy sales on the other part. Okay. I'm just giving you an example of what a 10 out of 10 of each of these would look like so you can kind of build these into like so you can think with this information. Okay. Conviction, if you're like, no matter who signs up for this thing, they're going to get amazing results. Like your life is going to change if you do this program. Like it's going to change. Like you just need to be ready for this. Like all your clothes, you're going to have to throw them out. Like because like you're not going to fit into them anymore. Like are you okay with this? But the last three, so these two are a little bit less under control, but these three, the last three are the ones that are really under your control. So conviction, report, and then the pain and need. And one of the things that I want to just like, and this is just the last point and then like you understand how this whole thing works. Okay. Sales is about identifying the gap from an overarching standpoint. It's about identifying where they are, where they want to be and what the gap is. Okay. If someone is not aware of this gap, they have no need for your bridge. Okay. It's a huge point. Pain is something that's there to increase their awareness, increase their awareness that there's a gap between where they are and where they want to go. Now the pain is specifically like a gap of knowledge, a gap of motivation, a gap of like you want to have as many gaps as possible so that you can have a solution that's going to bridge all of them. Right? The more gaps you can establish, the more bridges you can have, and then the more they're going to realize that they're not only doing, I don't have the motivation, I don't have the knowledge, I don't have the where, you know, like I don't know how to, like I don't know how to work out right. I don't know how to eat right. I don't know how to go out to eat. I don't know how to travel. I don't know how to, like all these things, but I don't know how to stick with things, whatever it is, right? You need to make sure that they, they know that. And you have to get them to say it. It's not about you saying it, it's about them saying it. So it's kind of like inception, but like, you need to make sure that they understand that point, which is why we ask people, what's your goal? They tell you, and you're like, cool, what's stopping you from getting there? Right? What have you done in the past? X, Y, and C? Cool. Why did those work for you? And they're like, I don't know. And then you're like, well, maybe, I mean, you haven't stuck with this, or was it unstuck with a bull? And they're like, yeah, I guess it wasn't sustainable. Okay, so you need a more sustainable plan. Boom, bridge number one. Like, okay. Did you like, did someone call you every day? Would you like, or like, did you just fall off? Or like, you were sticking with it all the time and then it stopped working. They're like, I usually fell off. Okay. So maybe more accountability would help. Yes. Bridge number two. Okay.
